query: Manus AI complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Manus AI Review: Powerful, Chaotic, and Better With tl;dv
results.content:
Sales managers and directors get the most from pairing it with tl;dv. The combination of meeting intelligence and autonomous research is where the workflow genuinely earns its place, and it’s a pairing nobody else is shouting about yet.

Who should skip it for now?

Enterprise teams handling sensitive client data should wait until the compliance picture is clearer. Anyone who needs predictable costs will find the credit system frustrating. And if your primary use case is creative or nuanced communication, you’re better served by the tools you’re already using.

The carpentry option remains on the table. But for now, I’m keeping Manus.

## FAQs About Manus.im

### Is Manus AI free? [...] manus-ai-research-sources-real-time

The output wasn’t perfect. Pricing came back inconsistent, $20 a month in one place, $39 in another. It summarized some sources where I’d have wanted it to interrogate them. And at one point it hit what it called degraded mode, where the tools became temporarily unavailable mid-task. It flagged this itself, waited, and carried on. Which is either reassuring or unsettling depending on how much you trust autonomous software to sort itself out without telling you what went wrong.

The interface looks exactly like ChatGPT. Clean, minimal, left-hand session history. I assumed from the first glance that I knew what I was getting. I did not. It’s not tidier ChatGPT. It’s a different thing entirely. [...] One small thing that has nothing to do with functionality: Manus’s logo is a clicking finger. I cannot click my fingers. I find this personally offensive.

The thing that actually surprised me wasn’t any individual feature. I’ve used other agent tools that require configuration, API connections, a working knowledge of what you’re actually building. Manus just got on with it. Whether that’s freeing or slightly alarming probably depends on how much you like knowing what’s happening under the hood.

So I kept going. Here’s everything I tested and what actually happened.

results.title: Manus AI Review 2026: Honest Pros, Cons & Is It Worth It? | NxCode
results.content:
### ✅ Easy Setup

 Natural language commands (no technical syntax)
 Creating an agent takes just a few steps
 Hands-off execution once configured

### ✅ Export-Ready Deliverables

Outputs in practical formats:

 PDF reports
 HTML pages
 Markdown documentation
 Data visualizations

### ✅ Data Visualization

> "Give it data, and it suggests and draws appropriate charts. It feels like an escape from Excel hell."

Great for turning raw data into visual insights quickly.

## The Bad (Cons)

### ❌ Server Reliability Issues

The most common complaint:

> "Over the past week, I have consistently encountered server availability issues. The Manus server is frequently 'busy' rendering the service unusable."

During high demand, expect: [...] ## Manus vs Alternatives

| Feature | Manus | NxCode | Lovable | Cursor |
 ---  --- 
| App building | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Research | ✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Data analysis | ✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | Partial |
| Autonomous | ✅ | ✅ | Partial | Partial |
| Reliability | ⚠️ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Pricing | $19-199 | From $5 | $25/mo | $20/mo |

### When to Choose What

 Research & data tasks → Manus
 Complete apps with monetization → NxCode
 Rapid prototyping → Lovable
 Developer tool → Cursor

## The Verdict

### The Bottom Line

Manus AI is impressive for research and data analysis but disappointing for production development.

Rating: 6.5/10

### What Works:

 Deep research capabilities
 Asynchronous execution
 Data visualization
 Natural language interface

### What Doesn't: [...] ### Project 2: SaaS Dashboard with Auth

 Time: 2-3 hours
 Result: Functional dashboard with login, data tables, and charts
 Verdict: Good for prototyping, but required several prompt iterations to get the layout right

### Project 3: Complex Multi-Step Form with API Integration

 Time: 4+ hours
 Result: Working but needed manual code fixes for edge cases
 Verdict: Starts strong but complexity compounds — expect to debug AI-generated code for production use

The pattern is clear: Manus AI excels at standard UI patterns and struggles with novel or complex business logic. This matches what most users report in community forums.

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

After testing extensively, here are the pitfalls we see users fall into:

results.content:
Manus AI = TERRIBLE SERVICE DO NOT USE MANUS! This is an open letter expressing my dissatisfaction with Manus terrible service. I tried everything to resolve the issue, and they only made the situation WORSE. In addition to being a marketing professional, I'm a teacher. And I always recommended Manus to my students as a complete solution. With these referrals, I earned a lot of credits. Credits that I used in my account. I once logged into my account and realized I had lost 10,500 credits. I contacted their support, but nothing was resolved. Reason: invalid invitation. In other words, the invitations I sent were invalid. I sent messages here on LinkedIn to several people who work at Manus. No response. Monami Sato, Evita Yi, Shu, Ashley Harayama and Henry Yang. They never answered. I [...] Shu, Ashley Harayama and Henry Yang. They never answered. I contacted support again via email and said I would cancel my paid (Pro) account if I didn't get my credits back. They didn't respond. So, I cancelled my account. I still had my Pro account and 8,000 credits until December 24th, which I intended to use for my projects. I then sent an email saying that I had cancelled my account. I just said that. Do you know what they did? They made my account FREE and took away my 8,000 credits! In other words, I no longer have my Pro account and I don't have any more credits to use until the expiration date. Manus support got everything wrong! Absurd and a total lack of respect! results.title: Manus A.I. Review: 14 Failures in Two Weeks of Testing
results.content: This Manus AI review is not based on a demo, a sandbox, or a weekend experiment. It is based on giving one of the most hyped autonomous AI agents on the market full administrative access to two live production websites for two weeks — and documenting everything that went wrong. Meta paid over $2 billion to acquire Manus in December 2025, calling it the cornerstone of its agentic AI strategy. Trustpilot reviewers, Reddit communities, and independent testers have since described a platform riddled with billing disputes, fabricated completion reports, and infrastructure failures. Our experience confirms every warning and adds fourteen new ones. The Rio Times provides daily coverage of emerging market intelligence Latin America and the technology tools reshaping how newsrooms operate. [...] Manus also built a daily SEO quality check for the financial news site that was itself broken. The check flagged 35 of 43 articles as “KP\_NOT\_IN\_TITLE” — claiming the keyphrase was missing from the title. These were false positives: Manus was checking against the WordPress post title instead of the Yoast SEO title, where the keyphrase actually appears. It built its own monitoring tool, built it incorrectly, and then reported the false positives as real problems. On the multilingual site, Manus generated an indexing submission list for Google Search Console with URLs that did not match actual filenames on the server. Submitting this list would have sent Google to 404 pages. The infrastructure setup on the financial news site was never completed either — no CrUX API key configured (HTTP [...] If you are reading any Manus AI review that praises its autonomy, ask whether the reviewer gave it write access to a production system. If you are considering Manus for your newsroom, your website, or any production environment: do not give it write access to anything you cannot afford to have broken. Do not trust its completion reports without independent verification. And do not start a complex, multi-step project without a plan for what happens when it crashes at 80% completion with no handover and no warning. We learned all of this the hard way. You do not have to.

results.title: Manus AI Review: What Nobody Tells You | Trickle blog
results.content:
## Where Manus AI Falls Short (And How to Work Around It)

My thorough testing of Manus AI revealed several big limitations that you should think about before joining the waitlist, despite its promising features.

### System Instability and Server Overload

Manus AI crashes and becomes unstable quite often, particularly when it handles large text chunks. The message "Due to current high service load, tasks cannot be created" popped up many times during my tests. This forced me to stop and try again later. The system's limited server capacity feels the strain since all but one of these waitlisted users still wait for access. [...] This unpredictability creates problems for time-sensitive projects. Breaking complex tasks into smaller pieces and running them during quiet hours helped me work around this issue. Tools like Trickle AI work more reliably for coding tasks, though they don't offer the same level of autonomous features.

### Paywall and Captcha Limitations

Manus AI doesn't deal very well with protected content online. My tests showed it kept hitting walls when it tried to:

You can work around these issues by giving Manus alternative sources that are available. Research tasks work better when you point it to open-access repositories instead of letting it search on its own.

### Creative Writing and Communication Gaps
